title: quararibea funebris subsp. funebris
intro: Quararibea funebris subsp. funebris, also known as the funereal quararibea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quararibea funebris subsp. funebris include its potential to treat fever, pain, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Herbal preparations of Quararibea funebris subsp. funebris include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are made from the plant's leaves and bark, and are often used to treat various health conditions.
